subject: Geography
title: The Alps: Heart of Europe
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Alps: Heart of Europe usable.

hook.text: The Alps stretch across eight countries, forming a 1,200 km arc through Europe.

buildup.text: They formed when Africa pushed into Europe, crumpling the crust between them.

discovery.text: Mont Blanc, at 4,808 meters, is Western Europe's highest peak.

twist.text: Hannibal marched elephants across the Alps in 218 BCE to surprise Rome.

climax.text: Alpine glaciers have lost half their volume since 1900 and may vanish by 2100.

punchline.text: Eight countries share one mountain wall.

quiz.question: How many countries do the Alps stretch across?
